chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI Cr. Bail Appl.No.3047 of 2025 Date Order with signature of the Judge Present: Mr. Justice Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro. Mr. Justice Syed Fiaz ul Hassan Shah. Irfan..............Vs........................ The State 17.12.2025. Mr. Khalid Hussain Solangi, Advocates for applicant Mr. Ali Haider Saleem, Addl. P.G. O R D E R = MUHAMMAD IQBAL KALHORO J: Applicant was arrested on 27.03.2025 by a police team of P.S. Quaidabad, Karachi headed by SIP Akhtiar Ali, from Mehran Highway Road near Chawal Godown Curve, Sherpao Colony, Landhi, Karachi on suspicious condition and from him 1050 grams of Charas was recovered, hence he was booked in FIR bearing Cr. No.163/2025 U/s 9 3(c), Sindh, CNS Act, 2024 of P.S. Quaidabad, Karachi. 2. Learned counsel in defence submits that applicant has been falsely implicated in this case; the investigation is complete; challan has been submitted and the alleged offence does not fall within prohibitory clause of section 497 Cr.P.C. 3. On the other hand, learned D.P.G. has opposed the bail stating that other case u/s 23(i), A, Sindh Arms Act has been registered against the applicant. 4. We have considered arguments of the parties and perused material available on record. The offence does not fall within prohibitory clause of section 497 Cr.P.C.; as far as other case is concerned, the applicant has been granted bail in that case and learned defence counsel has placed on record certified copy of order dated 20.12.2024; more so since challan has been submitted, applicant is no more required for further investigation. Accordingly, this application is allowed and applicant is granted bail subject to his furnishing a solvent surety in the sum of Rs.100,000/- and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the trail court.

The offence does not fall within prohibitory clause of section 497 Cr.P.C.; as far as other case is concerned, the applicant has been granted bail in that case and learned defence counsel has placed on record certified copy of order dated 20.12.2024; more so since challan has been submitted, applicant is no more required for further investigation. Accordingly, this application is allowed and applicant is granted bail subject to his furnishing a solvent surety in the sum of Rs.100,000/- and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the trail court. The observations made hereinabove are tentative in nature and would not prejudice case of either party at trial. The Cr. Bail Application is disposed of. JUDGE JUDGE A.K
